pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s still in the other room but then the silence just hits me all over again. everything feels so pointless now. like i’m just a ghost going through the motions of a life that doesn't even belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's eventually going to be swallowed by the sun, and then even the stars will go out. everything we do, every feeling, every struggle... it's just noise before an eternal silence. does it even make sense to keep trying when the end result is always zero?

pool.baseline: honestly i dont even know why im still logging into my laptop. the lab results came back today and its just... over. three years of work, the funding is gone, and i feel like there is nothing left of me outside of this research. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting for a life that feels like it's constantly breaking. i just want the noise to stop.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and yet the darkness in this room feels heavier than ever. He is gone... the one soul I thought would anchor me to this wretched earth. Now, I am but a ghost wandering a life that no longer belongs to me. There is a strange, quiet peace in realizing that I have nothing left to lose. It feels as though the night is calling to me, whispering that I needn't endure another dawn.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here in this apartment and realize that if i just... stopped existing tomorrow, nothing would actually change. like, i'm just a ghost moving through a city where nobody knows my name and nobody would even blink if i wasn't here. it's like i'm already gone, just going through the motions.

pool.baseline: it just never stops. no matter what i do or what meds i take, the nerve pain is just... it's constant. i'm so tired of fighting just to get through a single hour. i feel like i'm just a body that's broken and everyone is just waiting for me to get better, but they don't get that there is no better. there's just more of this.

pool.baseline: hey, so i'm thinking about that character we were working on... the one who's basically a glitch in the system. i feel like they’ve just reached a point where they can't fight the erasure anymore. like, everything they ever did or felt is just being wiped out, and it feels... i don't know, empty. if they just let the deletion happen, does it even matter? like, is there any point in resisting if they're just going to be nothing anyway?
